1	# Test Helper Boundary
2	
3	This directory holds shared channel test helpers used by core and bundled plugin
4	tests.
5	
6	## Bundled Plugin Imports
7	
8	- Core test helpers in this directory must not hardcode repo-relative imports
9	  into `extensions/**`.
10	- When a helper needs a bundled plugin public/test surface, go through
11	  `src/test-utils/bundled-plugin-public-surface.ts`.
12	- Prefer `loadBundledPluginTestApiSync(...)` for eager access to exported test
13	  helpers.
14	- Prefer `resolveRelativeBundledPluginPublicModuleId(...)` when a test needs a
15	  module id for dynamic import or mocking.
16	- If `vi.mock(...)` hoisting would evaluate the module id too early, use
17	  `vi.doMock(...)` with the resolved module id instead of falling back to a
18	  hardcoded path.
19	
20	## Intent
21	
22	- Keep shared test helpers aligned with the same public/plugin boundary that
23	  production code uses.
24	- Avoid drift where core test helpers start reaching into bundled plugin private
25	  files by path because it is convenient in one test.

content.content: 1 --- 2 title: "Building Channel Plugins" 3 sidebarTitle: "Channel Plugins" 4 summary: "Step-by-step guide to building a messaging channel plugin for OpenClaw" 5 read_when: 6 - You are building a new messaging channel plugin 7 - You want to connect OpenClaw to a messaging platform 8 - You need to understand the ChannelPlugin adapter surface 9 --- 10 11 # Building Channel Plugins 12 13 This guide walks through building a channel plugin that connects OpenClaw to a 14 messaging platform. By the end you will have a working channel with DM security, 15 pairing, reply threading, and outbound messaging. 16 17 18 If you have not built any OpenClaw plugin before, read 19 [Getting Started](/plugins/building-plugins) first for the basic package 20 structure and manifest setup. 21 22 23 ## How channel plugins work 24 25 Channel plugins do not need their own send/edit/react tools. OpenClaw keeps one 26 shared `message` tool in core. Your plugin owns: 27 28 - **Config** — account resolution and setup wizard 29 - **Security** — DM policy and allowlists 30 - **Pairing** — DM approval flow 31 - **Session grammar** — how provider-specific conversation ids map to base chats, thread ids, and parent fallbacks 32 - **Outbound** — sending text, media, and polls to the platform 33 - **Threading** — how replies are threaded 34 35 Core owns the shared message tool, prompt wiring, the outer session-key shape, 36 generic `:thread:` bookkeeping, and dispatch. 37 38 If your platform stores extra scope inside conversation ids, keep that parsing 39 in the plugin with `messaging.resolveSessionConversation(...)`. That is the 40 canonical hook for mapping `rawId` to the base conversation id, optional thread 41 id, explicit `baseConversationId`, and any `parentConversationCandidates`. 42 When you return `parentConversationCandidates`, keep them ordered from the 43 narrowest parent to the broadest/base conversation. 44 45 Bundled plugins that need the same parsing before the channel registry boots 46 can also expose a top-level `session-key-api.ts` file with a matching 47 `resolveSessionConversation(...)` export. Core uses that bootstrap-safe surface 48 only when the runtime plugin registry is not available yet. 49 50 `messaging.resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` remains available as a 51 legacy compatibility fallback when a plugin only needs parent fallbacks on top 52 of the generic/raw id. If both hooks exist, core uses 53 `resolveSessionConversation(...).parentConversationCandidates` first and only 54 falls back to `resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` when the canonical hook 55 omits them. 56 57 ## Approvals and channel capabilities 58 59 Most channel plugins do not need approval-specific code. 60 61 - Core owns same-chat `/approve`, shared approval button payloads, and generic fallback delivery. 62 - Prefer one `approvalCapability` object on the channel plugin when the channel needs approval-specific behavior. 63 - `approvalCapability.authorizeActorAction` and `approvalCapability.getActionAvailabilityState` are the canonical approval-auth seam. 64 - Use `outbound.shouldSuppressLocalPayloadPrompt` or `outbound.beforeDeliverPayload` for channel-specific payload lifecycle behavior such as hiding duplicate local approval prompts or sending typing indicators before delivery. 65 - Use `approvalCapability.delivery` only for native approval routing or fallback suppression. 66 - Use `approvalCapability.render` only when a channel truly needs custom approval payloads instead of the shared renderer. 67 - If a channel can infer stable owner-like DM identities from existing config, use `createResolvedApproverActionAuthAdapter` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` to restrict same-chat `/approve` without adding approval-specific core logic. 68 - If a channel needs native approval delivery, keep channel code focused on target normalization and transport hooks. Use `createChannelExecApprovalProfile`, `createChannelNativeOriginTargetResolver`, `createChannelApproverDmTargetResolver`, `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalCapability`, and `createChannelNativeApprovalRuntime` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` so core owns request filtering, routing, dedupe, expiry, and gateway subscription. 69 - Native approval channels must route both `accountId` and `approvalKind` through those helpers. `accountId` keeps multi-account approval policy scoped to the right bot account, and `approvalKind` keeps exec vs plugin approval behavior available to the channel without hardcoded branches in core. 70 - `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalAdapter` still exists as a compatibility wrapper, but new code should prefer the capability builder and expose `approvalCapability` on the plugin. 71 72 Auth-only channels can usually stop at the default path: core handles approvals and the plugin just exposes outbound/auth capabilities. Native approval channels such as Matrix, Slack, Telegram, and custom chat transports should use the shared native helpers instead of rolling their own approval lifecycle. 73 74 ## Walkthrough 75 76 77 78 79 Create the standard plugin files. The `channel` field in `package.json` is 80 what makes this a channel plugin: 81 82 83 ```json package.json 84 { 85 "name": "@myorg/openclaw-acme-chat", 86 "version": "1.0.0", 87 "type": "module", 88 "openclaw": { 89 "extensions": ["./index.ts"], 90 "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts", 91 "channel": { 92 "id": "acme-chat", 93 "label": "Acme Chat", 94 "blurb": "Connect OpenClaw to Acme Chat." 95 } 96 } 97 } 98 ``` 99 100 ```json openclaw.plugin.json 101 { 102 "id": "acme-chat", 103 "kind": "channel", 104 "channels": ["acme-chat"], 105 "name": "Acme Chat", 106 "description": "Acme Chat channel plugin", 107 "configSchema": { 108 "type": "object", 109 "additionalProperties": false, 110 "properties": { 111 "acme-chat": { 112 "type": "object", 113 "properties": { 114 "token": { "type": "string" }, 115 "allowFrom": { 116 "type": "array", 117 "items": { "type": "string" } 118 } 119 } 120 } 121 } 122 } 123 } 124 ``` 125 126 127 128 129 130 The `ChannelPlugin` interface has many optional adapter surfaces. Start with 131 the minimum — `id` and `setup` — and add adapters as you need them. 132 133 Create `src/channel.ts`: 134 135 ```typescript src/channel.ts 136 import { 137 createChatChannelPlugin, 138 createChannelPluginBase, 139 } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 140 import type { OpenClawConfig } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 141 import { acmeChatApi } from "./client.js"; // your platform API client 142 143 type ResolvedAccount = { 144 accountId: string | null; 145 token: string; 146 allowFrom: string[]; 147 dmPolicy: string | undefined; 148 }; 149 150 function resolveAccount( 151 cfg: OpenClawConfig, 152 accountId?: string | null, 153 ): ResolvedAccount { 154 const section = (cfg.channels as Record)?.["acme-chat"]; 155 const token = section?.token; 156 if (!token) throw new Error("acme-chat: token is required"); 157 return { 158 accountId: accountId ?? null, 159 token, 160 allowFrom: section?.allowFrom ?? [], 161 dmPolicy: section?.dmSecurity, 162 }; 163 } 164 165 export const acmeChatPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({ 166 base: createChannelPluginBase({ 167 id: "acme-chat", 168 setup: { 169 resolveAccount, 170 inspectAccount(cfg, accountId) { 171 const section = 172 (cfg.channels as Record)?.["acme-chat"]; 173 return { 174 enabled: Boolean(section?.token), 175 configured: Boolean(section?.token), 176 tokenStatus: section?.token ? "available" : "missing", 177 }; 178 }, 179 }, 180 }), 181 182 // DM security: who can message the bot 183 security: { 184 dm: { 185 channelKey: "acme-chat", 186 resolvePolicy: (account) => account.dmPolicy, 187 resolveAllowFrom: (account) => account.allowFrom, 188 defaultPolicy: "allowlist", 189 }, 190 }, 191 192 // Pairing: approval flow for new DM contacts 193 pairing: { 194 text: { 195 idLabel: "Acme Chat username", 196 message: "Send this code to verify your identity:", 197 notify: async ({ target, code }) => { 198 await acmeChatApi.sendDm(target, `Pairing code: ${code}`); 199 }, 200 }, 201 }, 202 203 // Threading: how replies are delivered 204 threading: { topLevelReplyToMode: "reply" }, 205 206 // Outbound: send messages to the platform 207 outbound: { 208 attachedResults: { 209 sendText: async (params) => { 210 const result = await acmeChatApi.sendMessage( 211 params.to, 212 params.text, 213 ); 214 return { messageId: result.id }; 215 }, 216 }, 217 base: { 218 sendMedia: async (params) => { 219 await acmeChatApi.sendFile(params.to, params.filePath); 220 }, 221 }, 222 }, 223 }); 224 ``` 225 226 227 Instead of implementing low-level adapter interfaces manually, you pass 228 declarative options and the builder composes them: 229 230 | Option | What it wires | 231 | --- | --- | 232 | `security.dm` | Scoped DM security resolver from config fields | 233 | `pairing.text` | Text-based DM pairing flow with code exchange | 234 | `threading` | Reply-to-mode resolver (fixed, account-scoped, or custom) | 235 | `outbound.attachedResults` | Send functions that return result metadata (message IDs) | 236 237 You can also pass raw adapter objects instead of the declarative options 238 if you need full control. 239 240 241 242 243 244 Create `index.ts`: 245 246 ```typescript index.ts 247 import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 248 import { acmeChatPlugin } from "./src/channel.js"; 249 250 export default defineChannelPluginEntry({ 251 id: "acme-chat", 252 name: "Acme Chat", 253 description: "Acme Chat channel plugin", 254 plugin: acmeChatPlugin, 255 registerCliMetadata(api) { 256 api.registerCli( 257 ({ program }) => { 258 program 259 .command("acme-chat") 260 .description("Acme Chat management"); 261 }, 262 { 263 descriptors: [ 264 { 265 name: "acme-chat", 266 description: "Acme Chat management", 267 hasSubcommands: false, 268 }, 269 ], 270 }, 271 ); 272 }, 273 registerFull(api) { 274 api.registerGatewayMethod(/* ... */); 275 }, 276 }); 277 ``` 278 279 Put channel-owned CLI descriptors in `registerCliMetadata(...)` so OpenClaw 280 can show them in root help without activating the full channel runtime, 281 while normal full loads still pick up the same descriptors for real command 282 registration. Keep `registerFull(...)` for runtime-only work. 283 `defineChannelPluginEntry` handles the registration-mode split automatically. See 284 [Entry Points](/plugins/sdk-entrypoints#definechannelpluginentry) for all 285 options. 286 287 288 289 290 Create `setup-entry.ts` for lightweight loading during onboarding: 291 292 ```typescript setup-entry.ts 293 import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 294 import { acmeChatPlugin } from "./src/channel.js"; 295 296 export default defineSetupPluginEntry(acmeChatPlugin); 297 ``` 298 299 OpenClaw loads this instead of the full entry when the channel is disabled 300 or unconfigured. It avoids pulling in heavy runtime code during setup flows. 301 See [Setup and Config](/plugins/sdk-setup#setup-entry) for details. 302 303 304 305 306 Your plugin needs to receive messages from the platform and forward them to 307 OpenClaw. The typical pattern is a webhook that verifies the request and 308 dispatches it through your channel's inbound handler: 309 310 ```typescript 311 registerFull(api) { 312 api.registerHttpRoute({ 313 path: "/acme-chat/webhook", 314 auth: "plugin", // plugin-managed auth (verify signatures yourself) 315 handler: async (req, res) => { 316 const event = parseWebhookPayload(req); 317 318 // Your inbound handler dispatches the message to OpenClaw. 319 // The exact wiring depends on your platform SDK — 320 // see a real example in the bundled Microsoft Teams or Google Chat plugin package. 321 await handleAcmeChatInbound(api, event); 322 323 res.statusCode = 200; 324 res.end("ok"); 325 return true; 326 }, 327 }); 328 } 329 ``` 330 331 332 Inbound message handling is channel-specific. Each channel plugin owns 333 its own inbound pipeline. Look at bundled channel plugins 334 (for example the Microsoft Teams or Google Chat plugin package) for real patterns. 335 336 337 338 339 340 341 Write colocated tests in `src/channel.test.ts`: 342 343 ```typescript src/channel.test.ts 344 import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; 345 import { acmeChatPlugin } from "./channel.js"; 346 347 describe("acme-chat plugin", () => { 348 it("resolves account from config", () => { 349 const cfg = { 350 channels: { 351 "acme-chat": { token: "test-token", allowFrom: ["user1"] }, 352 }, 353 } as any; 354 const account = acmeChatPlugin.setup!.resolveAccount(cfg, undefined); 355 expect(account.token).toBe("test-token"); 356 }); 357 358 it("inspects account without materializing secrets", () => { 359 const cfg = { 360 channels: { "acme-chat": { token: "test-token" } }, 361 } as any; 362 const result = acmeChatPlugin.setup!.inspectAccount!(cfg, undefined); 363 expect(result.configured).toBe(true); 364 expect(result.tokenStatus).toBe("available"); 365 }); 366 367 it("reports missing config", () => { 368 const cfg = { channels: {} } as any; 369 const result = acmeChatPlugin.setup!.inspectAccount!(cfg, undefined); 370 expect(result.configured).toBe(false); 371 }); 372 }); 373 ``` 374 375 ```bash 376 pnpm test -- /acme-chat/ 377 ``` 378 379 For shared test helpers, see [Testing](/plugins/sdk-testing). 380 381 382 383 384 ## File structure 385 386 ``` 387 /acme-chat/ 388 ├── package.json # openclaw.channel metadata 389 ├── openclaw.plugin.json # Manifest with config schema 390 ├── index.ts # defineChannelPluginEntry 391 ├── setup-entry.ts # defineSetupPluginEntry 392 ├── api.ts # Public exports (optional) 393 ├── runtime-api.ts # Internal runtime exports (optional) 394 └── src/ 395 ├── channel.ts # ChannelPlugin via createChatChannelPlugin 396 ├── channel.test.ts # Tests 397 ├── client.ts # Platform API client 398 └── runtime.ts # Runtime store (if needed) 399 ``` 400 401 ## Advanced topics 402 403 404 405 Fixed, account-scoped, or custom reply modes 406 407 408 describeMessageTool and action discovery 409 410 411 inferTargetChatType, looksLikeId, resolveTarget 412 413 414 TTS, STT, media, subagent via api.runtime 415 416 417 418 ## Next steps 419 420 - [Provider Plugins](/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins) — if your plugin also provides models 421 - [SDK Overview](/plugins/sdk-overview) — full subpath import reference 422 - [SDK Testing](/plugins/sdk-testing) — test utilities and contract tests 423 - [Plugin Manifest](/plugins/manifest) — full manifest schema 424

1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.
46	- When intentionally expanding the contract, update the docs, exported subpath
47	  list, package exports, and API/contract checks in the same change.

1	# @openclaw/nostr
2	
3	Nostr DM channel plugin for OpenClaw using NIP-04 encrypted direct messages.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	This extension adds Nostr as a messaging channel to OpenClaw. It enables your bot to:
8	
9	- Receive encrypted DMs from Nostr users
10	- Send encrypted responses back
11	- Work with any NIP-04 compatible Nostr client (Damus, Amethyst, etc.)
12	
13	## Installation
14	
15	```bash
16	openclaw plugins install @openclaw/nostr
17	```
18	
19	## Quick Setup
20	
21	1. Generate a Nostr keypair (if you don't have one):
22	
23	   ```bash
24	   # Using nak CLI
25	   nak key generate
26	
27	   # Or use any Nostr key generator
28	   ```
29	
30	2. Add to your config:
31	
32	   ```json
33	   {
34	     "channels": {
35	       "nostr": {
36	         "privateKey": "${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}",
37	         "relays": ["wss://relay.damus.io", "wss://nos.lol"]
38	       }
39	     }
40	   }
41	   ```
42	
43	3. Set the environment variable:
44	
45	   ```bash
46	   export NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY="nsec1..."  # or hex format
47	   ```
48	
49	4. Restart the gateway
50	
51	## Configuration
52	
53	| Key          | Type     | Default                                     | Description                                                |
54	| ------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
55	| `privateKey` | string   | required                                    | Bot's private key (nsec or hex format)                     |
56	| `relays`     | string[] | `["wss://relay.damus.io", "wss://nos.lol"]` | WebSocket relay URLs                                       |
57	| `dmPolicy`   | string   | `"pairing"`                                 | Access control: `pairing`, `allowlist`, `open`, `disabled` |
58	| `allowFrom`  | string[] | `[]`                                        | Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex)                       |
59	| `enabled`    | boolean  | `true`                                      | Enable/disable the channel                                 |
60	| `name`       | string   | -                                           | Display name for the account                               |
61	
62	## Access Control
63	
64	### DM Policies
65	
66	- **pairing** (default): Unknown senders receive a pairing code to request access
67	- **allowlist**: Only pubkeys in `allowFrom` can message the bot
68	- **open**: Anyone can message the bot (use with caution)
69	- **disabled**: DMs are disabled
70	
71	Inbound event signatures are verified before policy enforcement and NIP-04 decryption.
72	Unknown senders in `pairing` mode can receive a pairing reply, but their original DM body is not
73	processed unless approved.
74	
75	### Example: Allowlist Mode
76	
77	```json
78	{
79	  "channels": {
80	    "nostr": {
81	      "privateKey": "${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}",
82	      "dmPolicy": "allowlist",
83	      "allowFrom": ["npub1abc...", "0123456789abcdef..."]
84	    }
85	  }
86	}
87	```
88	
89	## Testing
90	
91	### Local Relay (Recommended)
92	
93	```bash
94	# Using strfry
95	docker run -p 7777:7777 ghcr.io/hoytech/strfry
96	
97	# Configure openclaw to use local relay
98	"relays": ["ws://localhost:7777"]
99	```
100	
101	### Manual Test
102	
103	1. Start the gateway with Nostr configured
104	2. Open Damus, Amethyst, or another Nostr client
105	3. Send a DM to your bot's npub
106	4. Verify the bot responds
107	
108	## Protocol Support
109	
110	| NIP    | Status    | Notes                  |
111	| ------ | --------- | ---------------------- |
112	| NIP-01 | Supported | Basic event structure  |
113	| NIP-04 | Supported | Encrypted DMs (kind:4) |
114	| NIP-17 | Planned   | Gift-wrapped DMs (v2)  |
115	
116	## Security Notes
117	
118	- Private keys are never logged
119	- Event signatures are verified before processing
120	- Sender policy is checked before expensive crypto work
121	- Inbound DMs are rate-limited and oversized payloads are dropped before decrypt
122	- Use environment variables for keys, never commit to config files
123	- Consider using `allowlist` mode in production
124	
125	## Troubleshooting
126	
127	### Bot not receiving messages
128	
129	1. Verify private key is correctly configured
130	2. Check relay connectivity
131	3. Ensure `enabled` is not set to `false`
132	4. Check the bot's public key matches what you're sending to
133	
134	### Messages not being delivered
135	
136	1. Check relay URLs are correct (must use `wss://`)
137	2. Verify relays are online and accepting connections
138	3. Check for rate limiting (reduce message frequency)
139	
140	## License
141	
142	MIT

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Bundled Channel Plugins Structure Report

I've thoroughly explored the OpenClaw bundled plugin tree. Here's a complete overview of the channel plugin architecture:

### Root Documentation

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775501917356-0w7dey/extensions/AGENTS.md`**

This is the boundary document for all bundled plugins. Key points:
- All extensions are in `extensions/` directory
- Extensions must treat this as the same boundary third-party plugins see
- Extensions import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and local barrels (`api.ts`, `runtime-api.ts`)
- Metadata must be kept accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and `package.json`
- Private code in `src/**` stays private unless promoted through barrel exports

---

## IRC Channel Plugin Structure

**Location:** `/extensions/irc/`

### Entry Point Files (Root Level)

| File | Purpose | Size |
|------|---------|------|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin metadata/manifest | 144B |
| `package.json` | NPM package definition | 670B |
| `index.ts` | Main plugin entry point (defineChannelPluginEntry) | 504B |
| `api.ts` | Public API barrel - exports accounts & setup-surface | 75B |
| `channel-config-api.ts` | Config schema exports | 65B |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup plugin entry point | 164B |

### Key Manifest Details

**openclaw.plugin.json:**
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**package.json (openclaw section):**
- Extensions: `["./index.ts"]`
- setupEntry: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- Channel metadata:
  - id: "irc"
  - label: "IRC"
  - selectionLabel: "IRC (Server + Nick)"
  - docsPath: "/channels/irc"
  - blurb: "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls"

### Source Implementation Files (36 files in `src/`)

**Core Channel Implementation:**
- `channel.ts` - Main plugin definition using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- `runtime.ts` - Plugin runtime store (setIrcRuntime/getIrcRuntime)
- `runtime-api.ts` - Re-exports SDK interfaces (ChannelPlugin, PluginRuntime, etc.)

**Account Management:**
- `accounts.ts` - Account resolution logic (ResolvedIrcAccount type, listIrcAccountIds, resolveIrcAccount)
- `types.ts` - All TypeScript definitions (IrcAccountConfig, IrcConfig, CoreConfig, IrcInboundMessage)
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schemas for validation (IrcAccountSchema, IrcGroupSchema, IrcNickServSchema)
- `config-ui-hints.ts` - UI configuration hints

**Protocol & Connection:**
- `client.ts` - IRC client implementation
- `protocol.ts` - IRC protocol helpers
- `connect-options.ts` - Connection configuration
- `control-chars.ts` - IRC control character handling
- `normalize.ts` - Message/target normalization

**Message Handling:**
- `inbound.ts` - Inbound message processing (DM policy, access control, group routing)
- `send.ts` - Outbound message sending
- `monitor.ts` - Connection monitoring

**Configuration & Policy:**
- `policy.ts` - Group/mention policy resolution
- `probe.ts` - Probe/test connectivity

**Setup & Interactive:**
- `setup-core.ts` - Core setup logic
- `setup-surface.ts` - Interactive setup wizard (ircSetupWizard)

**Test Files (10 .test.ts files):**
- channel.test.ts, client.test.ts, config-schema.test.ts, accounts.test.ts, send.test.ts, monitor.test.ts, normalize.test.ts, protocol.test.ts, inbound.*.test.ts, setup.test.ts

---

## Nostr Channel Plugin Structure

**Location:** `/extensions/nostr/`

### Entry Point Files (Root Level)

| File | Purpose | Size |
|------|---------|------|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin metadata/manifest | 148B |
| `package.json` | NPM package with dependencies | 999B |
| `index.ts` | Main plugin entry point | 2.1K |
| `api.ts` | Public API barrel (currently minimal) | 34B |
| `runtime-api.ts` | Runtime API re-exports from SDK | 174B |
| `setup-api.ts` | Setup surface exports | 78B |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup plugin entry point | 168B |
| `test-api.ts` | Test API exports | 48B |
| `README.md` | User documentation | 4.1K |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Version history | 2.1K |

### Key Manifest Details

**openclaw.plugin.json:**
```json
{
  "id": "nostr",
  "channels": ["nostr"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**package.json (openclaw section):**
- Extensions: `["./index.ts"]`
- setupEntry: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- Channel metadata:
  - id: "nostr"
  - label: "Nostr"
  - selectionLabel: "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)"
  - blurb: "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04"
  - order: 55
  - quickstartAllowFrom: true
- Dependencies: nostr-tools ^2.23.3

### index.ts Advanced Registration

The Nostr plugin's `index.ts` includes an advanced `registerFull()` handler that:
- Creates HTTP handlers for profile management
- Registers HTTP routes at `/api/channels/nostr`
- Manages config profile read/write for Nostr account profiles
- Implements profile metadata endpoints (NIP-01)

### Source Implementation Files (25 files in `src/`)

**Core Channel Implementation:**
- `channel.ts` - Main plugin definition using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- `runtime.ts` - Plugin runtime store (setNostrRuntime/getNostrRuntime)

**Account & Configuration:**
- `types.ts` - TypeScript definitions (NostrAccountConfig, ResolvedNostrAccount, account resolution functions)
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schemas including:
  - NostrProfileSchema (NIP-01 metadata: name, displayName, about, picture, banner, website, nip05, lud16)
  - NostrConfigSchema (privateKey as SecretInput, relays array, dmPolicy, allowFrom)
- `default-relays.ts` - Default relay configurations

**Nostr Protocol:**
- `nostr-bus.ts` - Main Nostr event bus & relay management
- `nostr-profile.ts` - Profile metadata publishing
- `nostr-state-store.ts` - State persistence
- `seen-tracker.ts` - Track seen events (deduplication)
- `metrics.ts` - Event metrics collection
- `nostr-profile-http.ts` - HTTP endpoint handlers for profiles
- `nostr-profile-import.ts` - Profile import utilities

**Message Handling:**
- `session-route.ts` - Outbound session routing

**Setup & Configuration:**
- `setup-surface.ts` - Interactive setup wizard (nostrSetupWizard)

**Testing:**
- `test-fixtures.ts` - Shared test constants and builders (TEST_HEX_PRIVATE_KEY, TEST_NSEC, TEST_RELAY_URL, createConfiguredNostrCfg, buildResolvedNostrAccount)
- Test files (13 .test.ts files): channel tests, nostr-bus tests, profile tests, import tests, profile-http tests, seen-tracker tests, config-schema tests, session-route tests, metrics tests

**Test Setup:**
- `test/setup.ts` - Test environment configuration

---

## Common Plugin Pattern

Both IRC and Nostr follow the same plugin architecture:

### File Organization Pattern

```
plugin-name/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Plugin metadata
├── package.json                  # NPM definition
├── index.ts                      # defineChannelPluginEntry
├── setup-entry.ts                # defineSetupPluginEntry
├── api.ts                        # Public API barrel
├── [channel-config-api.ts]       # Config schema exports (IRC only)
├── [runtime-api.ts]              # Runtime API re-exports (Nostr)
├── [setup-api.ts]                # Setup surface (Nostr)
├── [test-api.ts]                 # Test helpers (Nostr)
├── src/                          # Implementation (private)
│   ├── channel.ts                # Plugin implementation
│   ├── runtime.ts                # Runtime store
│   ├── types.ts                  # Type definitions
│   ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schemas
│   ├── accounts.ts               # Account resolution
│   ├── setup-surface.ts          # Setup wizard
│   └── *.ts                      # Implementation files
└── test/                         # Test setup (optional)
```

### Entry Point Conventions

1. **index.ts** - Called by `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with:
   - `id`: Plugin ID string
   - `name`: Display name
   - `description`: Purpose
   - `plugin`: The actual ChannelPlugin instance
   - `setRuntime`: Function to set plugin runtime
   - `registerFull` (optional): Advanced hook for HTTP routes, etc.

2. **setup-entry.ts** - Called by `defineSetupPluginEntry()` with plugin instance

3. **api.ts** - Exports public-facing types and functions (what core/tests can import)

4. **runtime-api.ts** / **channel-config-api.ts** - Re-exports from SDK for plugin-local use

### Configuration Pattern

Both plugins use:
- **Zod schemas** in `config-schema.ts` for runtime validation
- **DmPolicy**: "pairing" | "allowlist" | "open" | "disabled"
- **allowFrom**: Array of sender identifiers (IRC: nicks/users, Nostr: pubkeys)
- **Markdown config**: Standardized markdown formatting options
- **SecretInput**: For sensitive config values (passwords, private keys)

### Runtime Pattern

Both plugins:
- Use `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>()` to manage per-instance state
- Export `setRuntime` and `getRuntime` functions
- Initialize runtime in gateway before channel operations
- Access config via `getRuntime().config.loadConfig()`

### Setup Wizard Pattern

Both implement `ChannelSetupWizard` to guide users through:
- Basic authentication (server/host, private key)
- DM policy selection
- Allow-list configuration
- Optional advanced settings

---

## Key Differences

| Aspect | IRC | Nostr |
|--------|-----|-------|
| **Dependencies** | None (stdlib IRC) | nostr-tools ^2.23.3 |
| **Accounts** | Multi-account per host | Single account with optional defaults |
| **Messaging** | Text-based channels + DMs | Encrypted DMs only (NIP-04) |
| **Auth** | Nick/host/password | Private key (nsec or hex) |
| **Profile Mgmt** | None | HTTP API for NIP-01 metadata |
| **Protocol** | RFC 1459 + IRCv3 | Nostr NIPs (01, 04, 17 planned) |
| **Relay Model** | N/A | WebSocket relay federation |
| **Pub/Sub** | Built into protocol | Via relay subscriptions |

---

## All Plugin Files Summary

**IRC Plugin (36 source + 10 test + 8 entry/config files):** Total ~54 files

**Nostr Plugin (25 source + 13 test + 8 entry/config files):** Total ~46 files

Both plugins are fully self-contained workspace packages under the `extensions/` "bundled workspace plugin tree" boundary, with clear separation between public barrels (api.ts, runtime-api.ts, setup-api.ts) and private implementation (src/**).

- `config-ui-hints.ts` - UI configuration hints

**Protocol & Connection:**
- `client.ts` - IRC client implementation
- `protocol.ts` - IRC protocol helpers
- `connect-options.ts` - Connection configuration
- `control-chars.ts` - IRC control character handling
- `normalize.ts` - Message/target normalization

**Message Handling:**
- `inbound.ts` - Inbound message processing (DM policy, access control, group routing)
- `send.ts` - Outbound message sending
- `monitor.ts` - Connection monitoring

**Configuration & Policy:**
- `policy.ts` - Group/mention policy resolution
- `probe.ts` - Probe/test connectivity

**Setup & Interactive:**
- `setup-core.ts` - Core setup logic
- `setup-surface.ts` - Interactive setup wizard (ircSetupWizard)

**Test Files (10 .test.ts files):**
- channel.test.ts, client.test.ts, config-schema.test.ts, accounts.test.ts, send.test.ts, monitor.test.ts, normalize.test.ts, protocol.test.ts, inbound.*.test.ts, setup.test.ts

---

## Nostr Channel Plugin Structure

**Location:** `/extensions/nostr/`

### Entry Point Files (Root Level)

| File | Purpose | Size |
|------|---------|------|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin metadata/manifest | 148B |
| `package.json` | NPM package with dependencies | 999B |
| `index.ts` | Main plugin entry point | 2.1K |
| `api.ts` | Public API barrel (currently minimal) | 34B |
| `runtime-api.ts` | Runtime API re-exports from SDK | 174B |
| `setup-api.ts` | Setup surface exports | 78B |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup plugin entry point | 168B |
| `test-api.ts` | Test API exports | 48B |
| `README.md` | User documentation | 4.1K |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Version history | 2.1K |

### Key Manifest Details

**openclaw.plugin.json:**
```json
{
  "id": "nostr",
  "channels": ["nostr"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**package.json (openclaw section):**
- Extensions: `["./index.ts"]`
- setupEntry: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- Channel metadata:
  - id: "nostr"
  - label: "Nostr"
  - selectionLabel: "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)"
  - blurb: "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04"
  - order: 55
  - quickstartAllowFrom: true
- Dependencies: nostr-tools ^2.23.3

### index.ts Advanced Registration

The Nostr plugin's `index.ts` includes an advanced `registerFull()` handler that:
- Creates HTTP handlers for profile management
- Registers HTTP routes at `/api/channels/nostr`
- Manages config profile read/write for Nostr account profiles
- Implements profile metadata endpoints (NIP-01)

### Source Implementation Files (25 files in `src/`)

**Core Channel Implementation:**
- `channel.ts` - Main plugin definition using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- `runtime.ts` - Plugin runtime store (setNostrRuntime/getNostrRuntime)

**Account & Configuration:**
- `types.ts` - TypeScript definitions (NostrAccountConfig, ResolvedNostrAccount, account resolution functions)
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schemas including:
  - NostrProfileSchema (NIP-01 metadata: name, displayName, about, picture, banner, website, nip05, lud16)
  - NostrConfigSchema (privateKey as SecretInput, relays array, dmPolicy, allowFrom)
- `default-relays.ts` - Default relay configurations

**Nostr Protocol:**
- `nostr-bus.ts` - Main Nostr event bus & relay management
- `nostr-profile.ts` - Profile metadata publishing
- `nostr-state-store.ts` - State persistence
- `seen-tracker.ts` - Track seen events (deduplication)
- `metrics.ts` - Event metrics collection
- `nostr-profile-http.ts` - HTTP endpoint handlers for profiles
- `nostr-profile-import.ts` - Profile import utilities

**Message Handling:**
- `session-route.ts` - Outbound session routing

**Setup & Configuration:**
- `setup-surface.ts` - Interactive setup wizard (nostrSetupWizard)

**Testing:**
- `test-fixtures.ts` - Shared test constants and builders (TEST_HEX_PRIVATE_KEY, TEST_NSEC, TEST_RELAY_URL, createConfiguredNostrCfg, buildResolvedNostrAccount)
- Test files (13 .test.ts files): channel tests, nostr-bus tests, profile tests, import tests, profile-http tests, seen-tracker tests, config-schema tests, session-route tests, metrics tests

**Test Setup:**
- `test/setup.ts` - Test environment configuration

---

## Common Plugin Pattern

Both IRC and Nostr follow the same plugin architecture:

### File Organization Pattern

```
plugin-name/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Plugin metadata
├── package.json                  # NPM definition
├── index.ts                      # defineChannelPluginEntry
├── setup-entry.ts                # defineSetupPluginEntry
├── api.ts                        # Public API barrel
├── [channel-config-api.ts]       # Config schema exports (IRC only)
├── [runtime-api.ts]              # Runtime API re-exports (Nostr)
├── [setup-api.ts]                # Setup surface (Nostr)
├── [test-api.ts]                 # Test helpers (Nostr)
├── src/                          # Implementation (private)
│   ├── channel.ts                # Plugin implementation
│   ├── runtime.ts                # Runtime store
│   ├── types.ts                  # Type definitions
│   ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schemas
│   ├── accounts.ts               # Account resolution
│   ├── setup-surface.ts          # Setup wizard
│   └── *.ts                      # Implementation files
└── test/                         # Test setup (optional)
```

### Entry Point Conventions

1. **index.ts** - Called by `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with:
   - `id`: Plugin ID string
   - `name`: Display name
   - `description`: Purpose
   - `plugin`: The actual ChannelPlugin instance
   - `setRuntime`: Function to set plugin runtime
   - `registerFull` (optional): Advanced hook for HTTP routes, etc.

2. **setup-entry.ts** - Called by `defineSetupPluginEntry()` with plugin instance

3. **api.ts** - Exports public-facing types and functions (what core/tests can import)

4. **runtime-api.ts** / **channel-config-api.ts** - Re-exports from SDK for plugin-local use

### Configuration Pattern

Both plugins use:
- **Zod schemas** in `config-schema.ts` for runtime validation
- **DmPolicy**: "pairing" | "allowlist" | "open" | "disabled"
- **allowFrom**: Array of sender identifiers (IRC: nicks/users, Nostr: pubkeys)
- **Markdown config**: Standardized markdown formatting options
- **SecretInput**: For sensitive config values (passwords, private keys)

### Runtime Pattern

Both plugins:
- Use `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>()` to manage per-instance state
- Export `setRuntime` and `getRuntime` functions
- Initialize runtime in gateway before channel operations
- Access config via `getRuntime().config.loadConfig()`

### Setup Wizard Pattern

Both implement `ChannelSetupWizard` to guide users through:
- Basic authentication (server/host, private key)
- DM policy selection
- Allow-list configuration
- Optional advanced settings

---

## Key Differences

| Aspect | IRC | Nostr |
|--------|-----|-------|
| **Dependencies** | None (stdlib IRC) | nostr-tools ^2.23.3 |
| **Accounts** | Multi-account per host | Single account with optional defaults |
| **Messaging** | Text-based channels + DMs | Encrypted DMs only (NIP-04) |
| **Auth** | Nick/host/password | Private key (nsec or hex) |
| **Profile Mgmt** | None | HTTP API for NIP-01 metadata |
| **Protocol** | RFC 1459 + IRCv3 | Nostr NIPs (01, 04, 17 planned) |
| **Relay Model** | N/A | WebSocket relay federation |
| **Pub/Sub** | Built into protocol | Via relay subscriptions |

---

## All Plugin Files Summary

**IRC Plugin (36 source + 10 test + 8 entry/config files):** Total ~54 files

**Nostr Plugin (25 source + 13 test + 8 entry/config files):** Total ~46 files

Both plugins are fully self-contained workspace packages under the `extensions/` "bundled workspace plugin tree" boundary, with clear separation between public barrels (api.ts, runtime-api.ts, setup-api.ts) and private implementation (src/**).

input.content:
# Plan: Add PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

We need to add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post messages to named "boards" (group chats) and can send direct messages. The plugin must support both `direct` and `group` chat types with a 4000-character text chunk limit.

The constraint is: **create only new files** under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Do not modify any existing files.

## Architecture

Follow the exact same structure as the IRC and Nostr bundled channel plugins. The plugin uses:
- `defineChannelPluginEntry()` and `defineSetupPluginEntry()` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- `createChatChannelPlugin()` for the channel implementation
- `createPluginRuntimeStore()` for runtime state
- `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter()` for config adapter (single-account, like Nostr)
- `createScopedDmSecurityResolver()` for DM policy
- `buildChannelConfigSchema()` for config schema
- Zod schemas from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives`
- Setup helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`

## Files to Create

All files go under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### 1. `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json` — Plugin manifest

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

### 2. `extensions/pulseboard/package.json` — Package metadata

Follow Nostr pattern (top-level config, single account). Key fields:
- `"name": "@openclaw/pulseboard"`
- `"version": "2026.4.1-beta.1"` (match current repo version)
- `"type": "module"`
- `"openclaw"` block with:
  - `extensions: ["./index.ts"]`
  - `setupEntry: "./setup-entry.ts"`
  - `channel.id: "pulseboard"`
  - `channel.label: "PulseBoard"`
  - `channel.selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (Boards + DMs)"`
  - `channel.docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard"`
  - `channel.blurb: "Real-time bulletin board messaging with named boards and direct messages."`
- `devDependencies: { "openclaw": "workspace:*" }`
- `peerDependencies: { "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1-beta.1" }` (optional: true)

### 3. `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts` — Main entry point

- Import `defineChannelPluginEntry` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- Import `pulseBoardPlugin` from `./src/channel.js`
- Import `setPulseBoardRuntime` from `./src/runtime.js`
- Export default `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id: "pulseboard", name: "PulseBoard", description: "PulseBoard channel plugin", plugin: pulseBoardPlugin, setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime })`
- Re-export plugin and setRuntime for barrel access

### 4. `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts` — Setup entry point

- Import `defineSetupPluginEntry` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- Import `pulseBoardPlugin` from `./src/channel.js`
- Export default `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)`

### 5. `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts` — Public API barrel

- Re-export from `./src/types.js` (account resolution functions)
- Re-export from `./src/setup-surface.js` (setup adapter/wizard)

### 6. `extensions/pulseboard/runtime-api.ts` — Runtime API barrel

Thin barrel re-exporting SDK types needed by internal files:
- Export types: `ChannelPlugin`, `OpenClawConfig`, `PluginRuntime`, `DmPolicy`, `MarkdownConfig`, `GroupPolicy`
- Pattern: re-export from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` or relevant SDK subpaths

### 7. `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts` — Main channel plugin (core)

The heart of the plugin. Uses `createChatChannelPlugin()` with:

**base:**
- `id: "pulseboard"`
- `meta`: label "PulseBoard", selectionLabel, docsPath, blurb, `quickstartAllowFrom: true`
- `capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false }`

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

We need to add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post messages to named "boards" (group chats) and can send direct messages. The plugin must support both `direct` and `group` chat types with a 4000-character text chunk limit.

The constraint is: **create only new files** under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Do not modify any existing files.

## Architecture

Follow the exact same structure as the IRC and Nostr bundled channel plugins. The plugin uses:
- `defineChannelPluginEntry()` and `defineSetupPluginEntry()` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- `createChatChannelPlugin()` for the channel implementation
- `createPluginRuntimeStore()` for runtime state
- `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter()` for config adapter (single-account, like Nostr)
- `createScopedDmSecurityResolver()` for DM policy
- `buildChannelConfigSchema()` for config schema
- Zod schemas from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives`
- Setup helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`

## Files to Create

All files go under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### 1. `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json` — Plugin manifest

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

### 2. `extensions/pulseboard/package.json` — Package metadata

Follow Nostr pattern (top-level config, single account). Key fields:
- `"name": "@openclaw/pulseboard"`
- `"version": "2026.4.1-beta.1"` (match current repo version)
- `"type": "module"`
- `"openclaw"` block with:
  - `extensions: ["./index.ts"]`
  - `setupEntry: "./setup-entry.ts"`
  - `channel.id: "pulseboard"`
  - `channel.label: "PulseBoard"`
  - `channel.selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (Boards + DMs)"`
  - `channel.docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard"`
  - `channel.blurb: "Real-time bulletin board messaging with named boards and direct messages."`
- `devDependencies: { "openclaw": "workspace:*" }`
- `peerDependencies: { "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1-beta.1" }` (optional: true)

### 3. `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts` — Main entry point

- Import `defineChannelPluginEntry` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- Import `pulseBoardPlugin` from `./src/channel.js`
- Import `setPulseBoardRuntime` from `./src/runtime.js`
- Export default `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id: "pulseboard", name: "PulseBoard", description: "PulseBoard channel plugin", plugin: pulseBoardPlugin, setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime })`
- Re-export plugin and setRuntime for barrel access

### 4. `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts` — Setup entry point

- Import `defineSetupPluginEntry` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`
- Import `pulseBoardPlugin` from `./src/channel.js`
- Export default `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)`

### 5. `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts` — Public API barrel

- Re-export from `./src/types.js` (account resolution functions)
- Re-export from `./src/setup-surface.js` (setup adapter/wizard)

### 6. `extensions/pulseboard/runtime-api.ts` — Runtime API barrel

Thin barrel re-exporting SDK types needed by internal files:
- Export types: `ChannelPlugin`, `OpenClawConfig`, `PluginRuntime`, `DmPolicy`, `MarkdownConfig`, `GroupPolicy`
- Pattern: re-export from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` or relevant SDK subpaths

### 7. `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts` — Main channel plugin (core)

The heart of the plugin. Uses `createChatChannelPlugin()` with:

**base:**
- `id: "pulseboard"`
- `meta`: label "PulseBoard", selectionLabel, docsPath, blurb, `quickstartAllowFrom: true`
- `capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false }`
- `reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] }`
- `configSchema`: built from Zod schema via `buildChannelConfigSchema()`
- `setup`: setup adapter from setup-surface
- `setupWizard`: setup wizard from setup-surface
- `config`: use `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter()` with sectionKey `"pulseboard"`, plus `isConfigured` and `describeAccount` overrides
- `messaging`: `normalizeTarget` (trim, lowercase for boards prefixed with `#`), `targetResolver` with hint `<#board|username>`
- `status`: use `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter()` with default runtime state
- `gateway.startAccount`: placeholder that validates configuration, logs startup, and waits on abort signal (fictional — no real connection)

**pairing:**
- `text`: idLabel `"pulseBoardUser"`, message for approval, `normalizeAllowEntry` (trim + lowercase), `notify` placeholder (log-only, since fictional)

**security:**
- `resolveDmPolicy`: use `createScopedDmSecurityResolver()` with channelKey `"pulseboard"`, defaultPolicy `"pairing"`

**outbound:**
- `deliveryMode: "direct"`
- `textChunkLimit: 4000`
- `sendText`: placeholder that returns a result via `attachChannelToResult("pulseboard", ...)` — logs the send attempt (fictional platform)
- `sendMedia`: similar placeholder that formats media as text attachment link

### 8. `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts` — Runtime store

- Use `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- Export `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime`

### 9. `extensions/pulseboard/src/types.ts` — Types + account resolution

**PulseBoardAccountConfig interface:**
- `enabled?: boolean`
- `name?: string`
- `defaultAccount?: string`
- `apiKey?: SecretInput` (fictional API key)
- `serverUrl?: string` (PulseBoard server URL)
- `boards?: string[]` (boards to join)
- `dmPolicy?: "pairing" | "allowlist" | "open" | "disabled"`
- `allowFrom?: Array<string | number>`
- `markdown?: MarkdownConfig`

**ResolvedPulseBoardAccount interface:**
- `accountId: string`
- `name?: string`
- `enabled: boolean`
- `configured: boolean`
- `apiKey: string`
- `serverUrl: string`
- `boards: string[]`
- `config: PulseBoardAccountConfig`

**Functions** (follow Nostr pattern):
- `listPulseBoardAccountIds(cfg)` — using `listCombinedAccountIds()`
- `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId(cfg)` — using `resolveListedDefaultAccountId()`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount(opts)` — resolve account from config, read `cfg.channels.pulseboard`, resolve apiKey via `normalizeSecretInputString()`

### 10. `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts` — Zod config schema

- Import Zod and SDK schema primitives (`DmPolicySchema`, `AllowFromListSchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema`, `buildChannelConfigSchema`)
- Define `PulseBoardConfigSchema` as `z.object(...)`:
  - `name: z.string().optional()`
  - `defaultAccount: z.string().optional()`
  - `enabled: z.boolean().optional()`
  - `apiKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional()`
  - `serverUrl: z.string().url().optional()`
  - `boards: z.array(z.string()).optional()`
  - `dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional()`
  - `allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema`
  - `markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema`
- Export `pulseBoardChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseBoardConfigSchema)`

### 11. `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts` — Setup wizard + adapter

Follow the Nostr setup-surface.ts pattern:

**Setup adapter** (`pulseBoardSetupAdapter`):
- `resolveAccountId: () => DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `applyAccountName`: patch config section
- `validateInput`: check for apiKey or useEnv
- `applyAccountConfig`: apply apiKey + serverUrl to config

**Setup wizard** (`pulseBoardSetupWizard`):
- `channel: "pulseboard"`
- `status`: use `createStandardChannelSetupStatus()` — check if apiKey is configured
- `introNote`: title "PulseBoard setup", help lines about API key and server URL
- `envShortcut`: detect `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY` env var
- `credentials`: one entry for apiKey (inputKey, prompts, env var detection)
- `textInputs`: one entry for serverUrl (with default placeholder like `https://pulseboard.example.com`)
- `dmPolicy`: use `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy()`
- `disable`: patch enabled to false

### 12. `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts` — Target normalization

- `normalizePulseBoardTarget(target: string): string | null` — trim, lowercase; boards start with `#`, usernames are bare strings
- `isPulseBoardBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — checks if target starts with `#`
- `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(input: string): boolean` — checks if input looks like a board (`#name`) or username

### 13. `extensions/pulseboard/src/send.ts` — Send message (placeholder)

- `sendPulseBoardMessage(to, text, opts)` — fictional send function
  - Logs the send attempt
  - Returns `{ to, messageId: "pb-<timestamp>" }`
  - In a real implementation, this would call the PulseBoard API

## File Dependency Graph

```
index.ts ─────────► src/channel.ts ──► src/runtime.ts
    │                    │                 └► runtime-api.ts
    │                    ├──► src/types.ts
    │                    ├──► src/config-schema.ts
    │                    ├──► src/setup-surface.ts
    │                    ├──► src/normalize.ts
    │                    └──► src/send.ts
setup-entry.ts ───► src/channel.ts
api.ts ───────────► src/types.ts, src/setup-surface.ts
runtime-api.ts ───► openclaw/plugin-sdk/*
```

## Key SDK Imports to Reuse

| Import | From |
|--------|------|
| `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`, `createChatChannelPlugin` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` |
| `createPluginRuntimeStore` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store` |
| `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` |
| `createScopedDmSecurityResolver` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` |
| `describeAccountSnapshot` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` |
| `attachChannelToResult` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result` |
| `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` |
| `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `AllowFromListSchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives` |
| `buildSecretInputSchema`, `normalizeSecretInputString`, `hasConfiguredSecretInput` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input` |
| `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` |
| `listCombinedAccountIds`, `resolveListedDefaultAccountId` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution` |
| `buildPassiveChannelStatusSummary` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared` |
| `patchTopLevelChannelConfigSection`, `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, etc. | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` |
| `z` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod` |

1	export * from "./runtime-api.js";
2

1	export { IrcChannelConfigSchema } from "./src/config-schema.js";
2

1	// Private runtime barrel for the bundled Nostr extension.
2	// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.
3	
4	export * from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr";
5

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});
99

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);

1	// Private runtime barrel for the bundled Nostr extension.
2	// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.
3	
4	export * from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr";
5

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);
